result: F-HOLO-3: a metric-integrity-clean SO(3)-connection task where trivial covariance entropy is at chance (0.557, GATE PASS) but the CLOSED CLIFFORD one-shot estimator reaches the oracle (1.000, no training), while a strong trained MLP stays ~chance (0.626). Flat vs curved with matched Haar edge marginals; separates only through loop products. Closed form = tree gauge-fix + cotree residual, ~2 us/sample, estimator==oracle tested identity.

NEW_EVALUATION_REQUIREMENT: the matched-marginal discriminating task is the ceiling any future auto-holonomy method must clear BEFORE being trusted (F-HOLO-2 made concrete)
novelty_classification.note: ON-MISSION: the closed-form instantaneous rotor rule (GAP #2, closed-form gauge un-twist) demonstrated on the task holonomy is actually necessary for — NOT the exact-solve/Cholesky devolution (this is a holonomy transport + loop closure, O(|E|), reusing rotor_holonomy).

guards_added: curvature_task::edge_marginals_match_between_classes (in-code metric-integrity guard: classes must have matched isotropic marginals or the gate is invalid)
example runtime Step-1 gate: trivial<=0.60 AND oracle>=0.90

source_changes: src/curvature_task.rs (NEW 380 loc): wheel generator, Haar/axis-angle/angle/log helpers, marginal-match + holonomy invariants
examples/auto_holonomy_dissociation.rs (NEW 353 loc): gate + A/B + flux sweep

gates.step2_ab: closed-Clifford 1.000 REACHES oracle 1.000, one-shot no training; strong-MLP 0.626; trivial 0.557
gates.perf: estimator ~2 us/sample (< 50 us budget); example 13.8 s wall, 3.1 MB RSS

integrity_note: F-HOLO-2 honored: the previous task was solvable by trivial entropy (0.944>0.894); this task's classes have PROVABLY matched Haar edge marginals (verified in code), so trivial entropy is at chance and holonomy's value is measurable. The strong-MLP arm (3x data, hidden 64, 400 ep -> 0.626) rules out the 'undertrained strawman' objection. estimator==oracle is a tested identity, not an assumption.
honest_scope: On the wheel the closed form RECOVERS the oracle exactly (cycle basis == planted plaquettes) rather than strictly approximating it. This is the EXACT gauge solve; a LEARNED compositional holonomy rule (GAP #2 proper) and a topology where estimator != oracle remain open.
environment_note: rustc 1.96.1; hymeko_clifford vendored; rayon. macOS 26.5.2 Apple M5 Pro CPU. Uncommitted (working tree dirty).
next_experiment_authorized: grid/torus curvature task with flux on a random plaquette subset (estimator strictly approximates oracle; SNR gap), OR the learned holonomy-native rotor rule (RotorMeshNet + entropy feedback) attached to THIS metric on a task where the tree-gauge solve is insufficient (per-node multi-scale twist).
next_experiment_proposal: Prefer the topology-mismatch grid first (cheap, still closed-form, establishes estimator!=oracle regime) before investing in the learned compositional rule (GAP #2), so the learned method is measured against a genuine estimator-oracle gap rather than a saturated ceiling.
